seo.title: Link Building for Dev Tools tools directory
seo.description: A developer-first directory of Link Building for Dev Tools tools and references.

content.intro: A curated directory of tools, platforms, and data sources designed to help developers and technical marketers build high-quality backlinks, create link-worthy technical assets, and monitor domain authority growth.

entries.name: Ahrefs Backlink Checker
entries.description: Industry-standard tool for analyzing competitor backlink profiles and identifying broken link building opportunities in technical documentation.
pros: Granular filtering by DR and traffic
cons: Steep learning curve for advanced filters

entries.name: Hunter.io
entries.description: Finds professional email addresses for technical editors and site owners to facilitate personalized outreach for guest posts and tool reviews.
pros: Chrome extension for on-page email finding
cons: Occasionally misses data for smaller niche blogs

entries.name: F5Bot
entries.description: Free service that monitors Reddit, Hacker News, and Lobsters for specific keywords, allowing for real-time participation in link-relevant discussions.

entries.name: GitHub Archive
entries.description: A dataset of all public events on GitHub, useful for creating data-driven blog posts and reports that naturally attract technical backlinks.
cons: Requires SQL knowledge to extract value

entries.name: Pitchbox
entries.description: An influencer outreach and content marketing platform that automates the workflow of finding and contacting site owners at scale.

entries.name: Chart.js
entries.description: Simple yet flexible JavaScript charting library used to build interactive data visualizations that serve as high-value linkable assets.

entries.name: Connectively
entries.description: The successor to HARO, connecting developers and technical experts with journalists looking for quotes and expert insights for articles.
pros: Direct access to high-authority media outlets
High success rate for technical expertise
cons: New credit-based system limits free usage

entries.name: Screaming Frog SEO Spider
entries.description: Desktop-based website crawler used to find broken outbound links on target sites to facilitate 'broken link building' campaigns.
pros: Can crawl thousands of URLs quickly

entries.name: SparkToro
entries.description: Audience intelligence tool that reveals which podcasts, social accounts, and websites a specific developer audience follows.
pros: Saves weeks of manual market research
cons: Does not provide direct contact info

entries.name: BuzzSumo
entries.description: Content analyzer that identifies the most shared and linked-to technical content for any given topic or domain.
pros: Excellent for identifying trending dev topics
Shows who shared specific technical articles
cons: Data can be noisy for very niche topics

entries.name: Google Search Console
entries.description: The definitive source for seeing which sites are currently linking to your dev tool and which pages are attracting the most links.
cons: Data is often delayed by 2-3 days

entries.name: Postman Public API Network
entries.description: A directory where developers can publish API collections, creating a high-authority profile and backlink to their documentation.
cons: Requires a functional API and Postman collection
Link is often no-follow depending on profile status
